title: Muhlenbergia pringlei
taxonomy: Muhlenbergia pringlei is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Muhlenbergia, which comprises a group of perennial and annual grasses native to various parts of the Americas. This species is further classified as Muhlenbergia pringlei (Vasey) Vasey ex Bush, indicating its unique taxonomic classification. The classification of Muhlenbergia pringlei is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower arrangement. In the APG IV system, Muhlenbergia pringlei is placed within the order Poales.

intro: Muhlenbergia pringlei, also known as Pringle's muhly, is a species of grass native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a possible treatment for wounds, infections, and inflammatory conditions such as arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia pringlei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
